goal: Build Proxy Coin — decentralized bandwidth marketplace with Android app, Go backend, Solidity smart contracts, and Base L2 token

waves.name: Backend Orchestrator Core
waves.description: Node registry, WebSocket server, Redis, metering publisher

waves.name: Android Core Services
waves.description: Foreground service, WebSocket client, proxy engine, resource monitor

waves.name: Integration + Points
waves.description: End-to-end proxy flow, points ledger, test harness

waves.name: HTTPS + Security
waves.description: HTTPS CONNECT tunnel, domain blocklist, server-side metering

waves.name: Attestation + Monitoring
waves.description: Play Integrity, enhanced resource monitor, config push

waves.name: Contract Testing + Deploy
waves.description: Full test suite, fuzz tests, Base Sepolia deployment

waves.name: Wallet + Merkle Backend
waves.description: WalletManager, KeystoreHelper, reward calculator, Merkle generator

waves.name: Claim Flow + Wallet UI
waves.description: Merkle publisher, claim proof API, wallet screen, onboarding

waves.name: Customer API
waves.description: Customer auth, proxy endpoints, usage tracking, rate limiting

waves.name: Anti-Fraud + Trust
waves.description: Fraud detector, IP intelligence, behavioral analysis, trust scoring

waves.name: Beta Features
waves.description: Onboarding, referrals, billing, geo-targeting, sticky sessions

waves.name: Polish + Production
waves.description: Security hardening, release build, API gateway, deployment, docs, E2E

features.name: Protocol: Create protobuf definitions
acceptance_criteria: node.proto with Register, Registered, Heartbeat, ConfigUpdate, EarningsUpdate messages
proxy.proto with ProxyRequest, ProxyResponseStart, ProxyResponseChunk, ProxyResponseEnd messages
All messages use proto3 syntax with appropriate field types
features.technical_notes: Reference ARCHITECTURE.md data flow section for message types. Use proto3 syntax. Package: proxycoin.protocol.v1

features.name: Backend: Init Go module + project structure
acceptance_criteria: go.mod with Go 1.22+ and module path github.com/gofullthrottle/proxy-coin/backend
internal/ packages: node, proxy, websocket, metering, reward, fraud, auth, customer, config
Makefile with build, test, lint, proto-gen targets
features.technical_notes: Follow standard Go project layout. Use cmd/ for binaries, internal/ for private packages, pkg/ for importable packages.

features.name: Android: Init Gradle project + Hilt DI
acceptance_criteria: android/ directory with proper Gradle project structure
build.gradle.kts with all dependencies from ANDROID-APP.md libs.versions.toml
Hilt modules: AppModule, NetworkModule, DatabaseModule, CryptoModule
features.technical_notes: Use version catalog (libs.versions.toml). Kotlin 2.0.21, Compose BOM 2024.12.01, Hilt 2.51.1.

features.name: Contracts: Init Foundry project
acceptance_criteria: foundry.toml per SMART-CONTRACTS.md (solc 0.8.24, optimizer 200 runs, cancun EVM)
OpenZeppelin contracts installed via forge install
features.technical_notes: Fuzz runs = 1000. RPC endpoints for base_sepolia and base_mainnet. Etherscan config for verification.

features.name: Infrastructure: Docker Compose dev environment
acceptance_criteria: PostgreSQL: proxycoin DB, proxycoin user, health check
docker-compose up starts both services successfully
features.technical_notes: Use alpine images for smaller size. Add .env.example with default values.

features.name: Scripts: Protobuf generation script
features.technical_notes: Requires protoc, protoc-gen-go, protoc-gen-kotlin. Add check for dependencies at script start.

features.name: Protocol: Compile + verify generated code
acceptance_criteria: Generated code compiles in both Go and Kotlin projects
features.technical_notes: Run script, verify output, fix any protoc issues.

features.name: Backend: Config package
acceptance_criteria: Loads from env vars: DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, BASE_RPC_URL, LISTEN_ADDR, WS_ADDR, JWT_SECRET
features.technical_notes: Use envconfig or manual os.Getenv. Keep it simple, no YAML config files.

features.name: Backend: Database migrations
acceptance_criteria: migrations/001_initial.up.sql with all 7 tables from BACKEND.md
Tables: nodes, metering (partitioned), earnings, claimable_rewards, customers, customer_usage, fraud_events, referrals
Migrations apply successfully against Docker Postgres
features.technical_notes: Use golang-migrate for migration runner. Partition setup for metering table. Include referrals table.

features.name: Android: Navigation + base screens
acceptance_criteria: NavGraph.kt with routes for all 6 screens
Empty Composable stubs for each screen
Bottom navigation bar (Home, Earn, Wallet, Settings)
App launches and navigates between screens
features.technical_notes: Use Compose Navigation. Material3 bottom nav. Define routes as sealed class.

features.name: Android: Room database setup
acceptance_criteria: EarningsEntity, MeteringEntity, TransactionEntity with all fields
EarningsDao, MeteringDao, TransactionDao with CRUD queries
DatabaseModule providing database instance via Hilt
Database created successfully on app launch
features.technical_notes: Room version from libs.versions.toml. Use KSP for Room compiler.

features.name: Android: Network module
acceptance_criteria: ApiService.kt interface with all endpoints from ANDROID-APP.md
OkHttpClient with logging interceptor in NetworkModule
Retrofit instance configured for JSON + protobuf
features.technical_notes: Use Moshi or Kotlinx Serialization for JSON. Protobuf converter for binary endpoints.

features.name: Backend: Node registry
acceptance_criteria: NodeFilter supports: country, region, status, min trust_score, network_type
Unit tests with mock DB or test containers
features.technical_notes: Node struct matches BACKEND.md schema exactly. Use pgx/v5 with connection pool.

features.name: Backend: Node selector algorithm
acceptance_criteria: Filter → Score → Weighted random from top 5 per BACKEND.md algorithm
Score weights: trust 0.4, load 0.3, residential 0.2, uptime 0.1
Unit tests with various node configurations
features.technical_notes: Weighted random selection prevents overloading single best node.

features.name: Backend: WebSocket server
acceptance_criteria: internal/websocket/server.go with HTTP upgrade handler at /ws
Protobuf message deserialization for all message types
features.technical_notes: Use gorilla/websocket. Each connection gets read/write goroutines. SendCh for outbound messages.

features.name: Backend: Connection pool + message routing
acceptance_criteria: ActiveReqs sync.Map tracking pending requests per connection
Route PROXY_RESPONSE messages to waiting request channels
features.technical_notes: Use channels for request/response correlation. Each pending request gets a response channel with timeout.

features.name: Backend: Redis integration
acceptance_criteria: Node status keys: node:{id}:status, node:{id}:requests, node:{id}:heartbeat
Session stickiness: session:{id} → nodeID with 5min TTL
All key patterns match BACKEND.md Redis Usage section
features.technical_notes: Use go-redis/v9. Connection pool with health checks.

features.name: Backend: Metering event publisher
acceptance_criteria: internal/metering/reporter.go publishing to Redis Stream metering_events
MeteringEvent struct with all fields from BACKEND.md
Published after each proxy response completes
Consumer group setup for Metering Service
features.technical_notes: Use XADD for publishing, XREADGROUP for consuming in Metering Service.

features.name: Android: ProxyForegroundService
acceptance_criteria: Persistent foreground service with dataSync type
START_STICKY return for restart on kill
Lifecycle: create notification → start foreground → connect WebSocket → listen
features.technical_notes: Use Hilt @AndroidEntryPoint. Inject WebSocketClient, ProxyEngine, ResourceMonitor. Coroutine scope tied to service lifecycle.

features.name: Android: WebSocketClient
acceptance_criteria: OkHttp WebSocket connecting to wss:// backend
Protobuf serialization/deserialization for all message types
REGISTER with device info on connect
features.technical_notes: Use OkHttp WebSocket API. State machine: connecting→connected→reconnecting→disconnected. Coroutine-based.

features.name: Android: ProxyEngine
acceptance_criteria: Execute HTTP requests from device network for each PROXY_REQUEST
Stream response back: ProxyResponseStart → ProxyResponseChunk[] → ProxyResponseEnd
Error handling: timeout, DNS failure, connection refused
features.technical_notes: Use separate OkHttpClient from API client (no auth interceptor). Stream response body.

features.name: Android: ResourceMonitor
acceptance_criteria: ProxyForegroundService pauses proxy when thresholds exceeded
features.technical_notes: Use BroadcastReceiver for battery changes. ConnectivityManager.NetworkCallback for network changes.

features.name: Android: BootReceiver
acceptance_criteria: Starts ProxyForegroundService if user had it enabled
features.technical_notes: Simple receiver, check preference before starting service.

features.name: Android: MeteringService
acceptance_criteria: Record bytes_in, bytes_out, latency_ms, success per request in Room DB
features.technical_notes: Supplementary to server-side metering. Use WorkManager PeriodicWorkRequest.

features.name: Backend: Proxy request handler
acceptance_criteria: Call NodeSelector to find optimal node
Send PROXY_REQUEST via WebSocket to selected node
Wait for response with configurable timeout
Handle: node unavailable, timeout, partial response, error
features.technical_notes: Use context with timeout. Channel-based response collection from connection pool.

features.name: Backend: Proxy response handler
acceptance_criteria: Assemble streamed ProxyResponseChunks into complete response
Forward assembled response to waiting handler
Handle partial responses and out-of-order chunks
features.technical_notes: Buffer chunks by request_id. Use chunk_index for ordering.

features.name: Backend: Points ledger
acceptance_criteria: Insert earnings records per node per hour
GET /v1/earnings returns earnings for a node (date range filter)
Points accumulate but are not yet claimable on-chain
features.technical_notes: Use earnings table. Aggregate from metering events. Simple reward formula: bytes * rate.

features.name: Android: Basic settings
acceptance_criteria: Settings screen with WiFi-only toggle (default on)
ProxyForegroundService reads settings reactively via Flow
features.technical_notes: Use DataStore Preferences. Compose Switch and Slider components.

features.name: Integration: End-to-end test harness
acceptance_criteria: Test script or Go test that validates full proxy flow
Start backend, simulate node WebSocket connection
Send proxy request to known URL (e.g., httpbin.org/get)
features.technical_notes: Can be Go integration test or shell script. Mock the Android node with a Go WebSocket client.

features.name: Infrastructure: Full dev Docker Compose
acceptance_criteria: Orchestrator, API, Metering services with Dockerfiles
features.technical_notes: Multi-stage Dockerfile for Go. Mount source code for dev. Use Air or similar for hot reload.

features.name: Backend: HTTPS CONNECT tunnel
acceptance_criteria: Establish TCP tunnel through WebSocket to Android node
Node opens TLS connection to destination
No MITM — never see plaintext HTTPS content
features.technical_notes: CONNECT proxy creates a raw TCP tunnel. Different from HTTP proxy. Needs new message type for tunnel bytes.

features.name: Backend: Domain blocklist management
acceptance_criteria: Categories per SECURITY.md: CSAM, malware, gov/mil, financial, healthcare
Rate-based blocking: >100 req/min to one domain per node
Server-side filtering before routing to node
features.technical_notes: Hash-based so nodes never see actual blocked domains. Bloom filter for fast lookup optional.

features.name: Backend: Blocklist distribution
acceptance_criteria: Send blocklist hash set on REGISTER and via CONFIG_UPDATE
Include version hash so nodes only download on change
Redis pub/sub for broadcast to all orchestrator instances
features.technical_notes: Compress hash set before sending. Delta updates for efficiency.

features.name: Android: HTTPS CONNECT in ProxyEngine
acceptance_criteria: Handle CONNECT-type proxy requests from WebSocket
Relay bytes through WebSocket tunnel bidirectionally
features.technical_notes: Use java.net.Socket for raw TCP. Different path from HTTP proxy in ProxyEngine.

features.name: Android: Domain filter
acceptance_criteria: DomainFilter class receiving blocklist from backend
Hash destination domain and check against blocklist set
Reject blocked domains with error response
All test blocked domains correctly rejected
features.technical_notes: SHA-256 hash of domain. HashSet for O(1) lookup.

features.name: Backend: Server-side metering (authoritative)
acceptance_criteria: internal/metering/counter.go counting WebSocket bytes in real-time
features.technical_notes: Count bytes at WebSocket frame level. Intercept read/write on connection.

features.name: Android: Play Integrity integration
acceptance_criteria: Graceful error handling (API unavailable, token generation failure)
features.technical_notes: play-integrity dependency from libs.versions.toml. Needs Google Play Services.

features.name: Backend: Attestation verification
acceptance_criteria: Verify via Google API: genuine device, genuine APK, not rooted
On failure: suspend earnings, log fraud_event, require re-attestation
Periodic re-check not just at registration
features.technical_notes: Google Play Integrity API server-side verification. Store attestation timestamp per node.

features.name: Android: ResourceMonitor enhancements
features.technical_notes: PowerManager.THERMAL_STATUS_* for thermal. ActivityManager.MemoryInfo for memory.

features.name: Backend: Node config push
acceptance_criteria: Push config updates via WebSocket CONFIG_UPDATE message
Config includes: earning rates, blocklist hash, max_concurrent, heartbeat interval
features.technical_notes: Admin endpoint to trigger config push. All orchestrator instances receive via pub/sub.

features.name: Android: Connection resilience
acceptance_criteria: Jitter added to reconnect backoff (prevent thundering herd)
features.technical_notes: Random jitter = random(0, backoff * 0.3). Store session token for resume.

features.name: Android: Dashboard screen
acceptance_criteria: Live stats: uptime, requests served, avg latency, trust score
features.technical_notes: Use a Compose charting library (Vico or YCharts). Pull data from ViewModel.

features.name: Android: DashboardViewModel
acceptance_criteria: Collect from ProxyForegroundService (connection state, live stats)
Collect from API (token price, trust score)
features.technical_notes: Combine multiple Flows with combine(). Handle loading/error states.

features.name: Android: Earnings screen
acceptance_criteria: Breakdown table: bandwidth, uptime bonus, quality bonus, referral
features.technical_notes: Reuse charting library from dashboard. Tab state with rememberSaveable.

features.name: Android: Settings screen (full)
acceptance_criteria: Bandwidth: max slider (10-100%), WiFi-only, cellular toggle, cellular cap
Power: battery threshold slider (10-50%), charging-only toggle
Notifications: milestones, status, weekly summary toggles
Account: device ID, node ID, trust score, referral code
About: version, ToS link, privacy link, licenses
features.technical_notes: Compose Slider, Switch components. Grouped sections with headers.

features.name: Android: Notification enhancements
acceptance_criteria: Rich notification: connection state, today's earnings, bandwidth, uptime
features.technical_notes: NotificationCompat.Builder with RemoteViews for custom layout.

features.name: Contracts: ProxyCoinToken.sol
acceptance_criteria: mint() with supply cap check, onlyRole(MINTER_ROLE)
features.technical_notes: Import from @openzeppelin/contracts. Exact code in SMART-CONTRACTS.md.

features.name: Contracts: RewardDistributor.sol
acceptance_criteria: Cumulative tracking: claimed mapping, claimable = cumulative - claimed
features.technical_notes: Double keccak256 for leaf to prevent second preimage attacks. Exact code in SMART-CONTRACTS.md.

features.name: Contracts: Staking.sol
acceptance_criteria: stake(amount) - determine tier, transfer tokens, set lockUntil
unstake() - check lock period, return tokens
slash(staker, reason) - SLASHER_ROLE, 50% slash, burn slashed tokens
features.technical_notes: SafeERC20 for transfers. IBurnableToken interface for slash burns. Exact code in SMART-CONTRACTS.md.

features.name: Contracts: Vesting.sol
acceptance_criteria: createSchedule() with beneficiary, total, start, cliff, duration, revocable
release() calculates vested amount, transfers releasable
revoke() returns unvested to owner (if revocable)
features.technical_notes: Exact code in SMART-CONTRACTS.md. SafeERC20 for transfers.

features.name: Contracts: Deploy script
acceptance_criteria: script/Deploy.s.sol deploying all 4 contracts in order
features.technical_notes: Use forge Script. Broadcast transactions. Verify deployment order.

features.name: Contracts: DistributeRewards script
features.technical_notes: Simple script. Pass root via --sig or env var.

features.name: Tests: ProxyCoinToken
acceptance_criteria: Test mint: success, exceeds max supply revert, unauthorized revert
Test role management: grant, revoke, renounce
features.technical_notes: Foundry test with setUp deploying token. Use vm.expectRevert for error cases.

features.name: Tests: RewardDistributor
acceptance_criteria: Test setMerkleRoot: owner success, non-owner revert, epoch increment
Test claim: valid proof success, invalid proof revert
Test partial claim then full claim
Generate test Merkle trees in Solidity
features.technical_notes: Build Merkle tree in test. Use murky library or manual tree construction.

features.name: Tests: Staking
acceptance_criteria: Test stake all 4 tiers with correct lock periods
Test unstake before lock (revert) and after lock (success)
Test slash: amount, burn, unauthorized revert
features.technical_notes: Use vm.warp to advance block.timestamp past lock periods.

features.name: Tests: Vesting
acceptance_criteria: Test createSchedule success and duplicate revert
Test release before cliff (0), during vest (proportional), after full vest
Test revoke: revocable success, non-revocable revert
features.technical_notes: Use vm.warp for time manipulation. Test with multiple beneficiaries.

features.name: Tests: Fuzz testing
acceptance_criteria: Fuzz tests (1000 runs) for mint amounts, claim amounts, stake amounts
Fuzz vesting calculations with random timestamps
features.technical_notes: Foundry fuzz with [fuzz] runs = 1000 in foundry.toml.

features.name: Contracts: Deploy to Base Sepolia
acceptance_criteria: All 4 contracts deployed to Base Sepolia testnet
Manual test claim succeeds on testnet
features.technical_notes: Need BASE_SEPOLIA_RPC and DEPLOYER_KEY. Use forge script --broadcast.

features.name: Android: WalletManager
acceptance_criteria: Store private key encrypted in Android Keystore
getBalance() queries ERC-20 balanceOf via Web3j
RPC via Alchemy/Infura for Base L2
features.technical_notes: Web3j 4.12.2 for Ethereum operations. BIP-39/BIP-44 via bitcoinj or custom implementation.

features.name: Android: KeystoreHelper
acceptance_criteria: Android Keystore wrapper for AES key generation
Clear sensitive data from memory after use
features.technical_notes: KeyGenParameterSpec with setUserAuthenticationRequired if biometrics available.

features.name: Android: Mnemonic + backup flow
acceptance_criteria: Generate BIP-39 mnemonic with proper entropy
Display words with 'write this down' warning
Confirmation: user selects words in correct order
Import flow: paste mnemonic or private key
features.technical_notes: Use BIP-39 word list. Validate checksum before accepting import.

features.name: Android: TransactionBuilder
acceptance_criteria: Build send tx: ERC-20 transfer(to, amount)
Gas estimation via Web3j gas oracle
Wait for 1 block confirmation on Base
Return TxHash on success, error on failure
features.technical_notes: Generate contract wrapper with Web3j CLI or manually encode ABI calls.

features.name: Backend: Reward calculator
acceptance_criteria: Aggregate metering data per node for the epoch
Apply multipliers: trust (0.5-2.0x), uptime (1.0-1.5x), quality (0.8-1.3x), staking (1.0-1.5x)
Unit tests with mock metering data
features.technical_notes: Use cron or ticker for hourly execution. Match formula from BACKEND.md CalculateRewards.

features.name: Backend: Merkle tree generator
acceptance_criteria: Get all pending rewards from claimable_rewards table
Build Merkle tree (binary tree, sorted pairs)
Store proofs in database per wallet
Return root hash for on-chain publishing
features.technical_notes: Match Solidity leaf encoding exactly: keccak256(bytes.concat(keccak256(abi.encode(addr, amount))))

features.name: Backend: Merkle root publisher
acceptance_criteria: Go Ethereum client (go-ethereum/ethclient) for Base L2
Signs and submits transaction with backend wallet
features.technical_notes: Need deployer private key or separate publisher wallet. Use abigen for contract binding.

features.name: Backend: Claim proof API
acceptance_criteria: GET /v1/rewards/proof returns Merkle proof for requesting node's wallet
Response: cumulativeAmount, proof array, current epoch
GET /v1/rewards/history returns claim history with tx hashes
features.technical_notes: Proof is array of bytes32 hashes. Return as hex strings.

features.name: Android: Wallet screen
acceptance_criteria: Wallet address with QR code and copy button
Claim Rewards button: fetch proof → build tx → sign → submit
Send PRXY button with address input and amount
Token price from DEX (optional: placeholder OK)
features.technical_notes: Use ZXing or Compose QR library for QR code. Transaction list from Room DB + API.

features.name: Android: WalletViewModel
acceptance_criteria: Coordinate WalletManager (on-chain balance), API (pending, proof), TransactionBuilder
WalletUiState: balance, pending, transactions, loading, error
Claim flow: fetch proof → build tx → sign → submit → wait confirm → refresh
Send flow: validate address → build tx → sign → submit → refresh
Error handling for insufficient gas, failed tx
features.technical_notes: Use viewModelScope for coroutines. Handle all error states gracefully.

features.name: Android: WalletSetupScreen
acceptance_criteria: Create New Wallet: generate mnemonic → show backup → confirm words
Import Existing: paste mnemonic or private key → validate → import
Wallet address display with copy button after setup
Navigate to Dashboard after wallet setup
Persist wallet creation state (skip on subsequent launches)
features.technical_notes: Part of onboarding flow. Guard: if wallet exists, skip this screen.

features.name: Integration: End-to-end claim flow
acceptance_criteria: Test: node earns rewards → calculator runs → Merkle generated → root published
Node submits claim transaction to Base Sepolia
Verify PRXY tokens received in wallet
features.technical_notes: Integration test against Base Sepolia. May need funded test wallet.

features.name: Backend: Customer auth
acceptance_criteria: internal/auth/jwt.go: JWT generation (15 min expiry) and validation
internal/auth/apikey.go: API key generation (prxy_live_xxx format), bcrypt storage, rotation
Customer registration with email + bcrypt password
Middleware for JWT and API key authentication
features.technical_notes: golang-jwt/jwt/v5. API keys: crypto/rand for generation, bcrypt for storage.

features.name: Backend: Customer registration + login
acceptance_criteria: POST /v1/auth/register: email + password → create customer → return JWT
POST /v1/auth/login: email + password → verify → return JWT + refresh
GET /v1/auth/apikey: return or rotate API key
features.technical_notes: Standard REST auth pattern. Return 409 for duplicate email.

features.name: Backend: Proxy request endpoint
acceptance_criteria: Authenticate via API key or JWT
Validate request body: url, method, geo, headers, timeout
Route to Orchestrator for node selection and proxy execution
Return formatted response with node info + metrics
Error responses: 408 timeout, 503 no nodes, 429 rate limit
features.technical_notes: Bridge between Customer API and Orchestrator. Base64 encode response body.

features.name: Backend: Batch proxy endpoint
acceptance_criteria: POST /v1/proxy/batch accepts array of proxy requests
Same auth and validation as single endpoint
features.technical_notes: Use errgroup or worker pool. Return partial results if some fail.

features.name: Backend: Usage tracking
acceptance_criteria: Record per-customer daily usage in customer_usage table
features.technical_notes: Upsert daily record. Use COALESCE for incrementing counters.

features.name: Backend: Rate limiting
acceptance_criteria: Redis-based rate limiting per API key per day
Plan limits: Free 100, Starter 10K, Pro 100K, Enterprise custom
Return 429 with Retry-After header when exceeded
features.technical_notes: Use Redis INCR with EXPIRE. Sliding window or fixed window.

features.name: Backend: Fraud detector
acceptance_criteria: Self-proxy verification (verify backend is only traffic source)
Bandwidth inflation (server vs client byte divergence >10%)
Sybil detection (multiple nodes same IP range)
Log fraud_events with severity and details
Auto-actions: warning → trust reduction, critical → suspend/ban
features.technical_notes: Run as periodic job analyzing recent metering data. Alert on anomalies.

features.name: Backend: IP intelligence
acceptance_criteria: Classify IP: residential, datacenter, VPN, proxy
Use GeoIP database (MaxMind GeoLite2) + ASN analysis
Cross-reference claimed location vs IP geolocation
features.technical_notes: MaxMind GeoLite2 ASN database. Update monthly. oschwald/maxminddb-golang.

features.name: Backend: Behavioral analysis
acceptance_criteria: Detect sudden bandwidth spikes (>10x normal for node)
Detect no sleep cycles (24/7 = likely emulator)
Pattern correlation: multiple nodes from same IP range
New node ramp-up: 0.5x earnings for first 7 days
features.technical_notes: Analyze rolling 24h/7d windows. Statistical thresholds for spike detection.

features.name: Backend: Trust score calculator
acceptance_criteria: Weighted model per SECURITY.md: attestation 0.15, uptime 0.20, verification 0.25, IP 0.15, bandwidth 0.15, age 0.10
features.technical_notes: Store in nodes table trust_score column. Update via cron or after metering aggregation.

features.name: Backend: Fraud event logging
acceptance_criteria: Store fraud events in fraud_events table
Fields: node_id, event_type, severity, details (JSONB), action_taken
features.technical_notes: Simple INSERT. Admin API for viewing/resolving fraud events.

features.name: Backend: Spot-check verification
acceptance_criteria: Send known URLs to nodes periodically
features.technical_notes: Maintain list of test URLs with known response hashes. Run via goroutine ticker.

features.name: Android: Onboarding flow
acceptance_criteria: WelcomeScreen: logo, tagline, value props, Get Started CTA
PermissionsScreen: battery optimization, notifications with explanations
Skip to Dashboard if already completed
features.technical_notes: Use HorizontalPager for swipeable onboarding. Request permissions via ActivityResultContracts.

features.name: Android: Referral screen
acceptance_criteria: Referral stats: count, total earnings from referrals
features.technical_notes: Use Android sharesheet for sharing referral link.

features.name: Backend: Referral system
acceptance_criteria: Generate unique referral codes per node
5% of referee earnings shared to referrer
features.technical_notes: Auto-generate code on node registration. Use nanoid for short codes.

features.name: Backend: Billing system
acceptance_criteria: Invoice generation: period, requests, bytes, cost
Plan limits enforced in rate limiting
features.technical_notes: Simple billing without payment gateway for now. Track cost_usd in customer_usage.

features.name: Backend: Geographic targeting
acceptance_criteria: NodeSelector supports country + region targeting from customer requests
GET /v1/status: available node counts by country/region
Customers can check coverage before requesting
features.technical_notes: Extend existing NodeSelector filter. Aggregate node counts from registry.

features.name: Backend: Sticky sessions
acceptance_criteria: session:{sessionID} → nodeID in Redis with 5min TTL
Customer sends session_id in proxy request
Selector checks Redis for existing binding
Route to same node if available, else new node + update binding
features.technical_notes: Simple Redis GET/SET with TTL. Fall back to normal selection if bound node offline.

features.name: Android: Security hardening
acceptance_criteria: Root detection: su binary, Magisk, system modifications
Emulator detection: build props, sensor count, telephony
Memory zeroing of sensitive data (keys, mnemonics)
features.technical_notes: CertificatePinner for API domain. Check multiple root indicators.

features.name: Android: Release build config
acceptance_criteria: Strip all Log calls in release builds
features.technical_notes: Keep Hilt, Room, Retrofit proguard rules. Strip Log.d/Log.v in release.

features.name: Backend: API Gateway config
acceptance_criteria: Nginx or Caddy config for API gateway
features.technical_notes: infrastructure/nginx/nginx.conf. Separate upstream blocks for each service.

features.name: Infrastructure: Production deployment
acceptance_criteria: Production Docker Compose or K8s manifests
Service health checks and resource limits
Separate configs for orchestrator, api, metering
Docker images build and run correctly
features.technical_notes: Multi-stage Dockerfiles for small images. Health check endpoints per service.

features.name: Docs: API reference + legal
acceptance_criteria: docs/api-reference.md with all Customer API endpoints
features.technical_notes: Legal docs are skeletons requiring legal review. API docs from actual endpoint implementations.

features.name: Integration: Full system E2E tests
acceptance_criteria: Customer registers → gets API key → submits proxy request
Node receives and executes → response returned
Metering recorded → rewards calculated → Merkle generated
All tests pass with clean setup/teardown
features.technical_notes: Docker Compose test environment. Go integration tests or shell scripts.
